title: Ports, Protocols, Services, and Traffic Types
description: Build and diagnose a service-access matrix from client, firewall, resolver, server, and capture evidence. Learners cumulatively produce a complete first version of a verified protocol and service matrix while every earlier competency remains active through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Associate standard ports with purpose, security, and transport behavior.
Trace name, address, session, and application dependencies instead of memorizing isolated numbers.
Distinguish traffic delivery types and tunneling or security protocols.
